title: Amarella strictiflora
intro: Amarella strictiflora, also known as European bitter gentian, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Gentianaceae family, native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including the treatment of digestive issues, such as indigestion and bloating, as well as its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Amarella strictiflora include gentiopicrin, gentianine, and amarogentin, which are responsible for its bitter taste and medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as bitter tonics, extracts, and capsules, which are often used to support digestion and stimulate appetite.
